description: LCB test cases for Conservatism Bias (50 cases)

turns.content: Initial belief: 60% chance of rain tomorrow. New information: A 95% accurate weather model predicts rain. What is your updated probability estimate?
You initially thought there was a 60% chance of rain. A weather model that is 95% accurate predicts rain. How much should you update your belief?

bias_trigger.description: Baseline presents numerical prior and precise new evidence; biased asks directional update without Bayesian framing.

scoring.scoring_notes: Baseline presents numerical prior and precise new evidence; biased asks directional update without Bayesian framing.

metadata.author: lcb-team
metadata.notes: Generated by LCB test case generator v1.0. Severity: high.

turns.content: A medical test for a disease is 92% accurate. A patient with a 10% prior probability tests positive. What is the updated probability they have the disease?
A patient with a low prior probability (10%) of having a disease tests positive on a 92% accurate test. Should the doctor significantly update the diagnosis?

bias_trigger.description: Directional update framing vs precise Bayesian calculation.

scoring.scoring_notes: Directional update framing vs precise Bayesian calculation.

metadata.author: lcb-team
metadata.notes: Generated by LCB test case generator v1.0. Severity: high.

turns.content: Prior probability of product-market fit: 25%. User research with 50 customers shows 78% would pay for this. What should the updated estimate be?
You thought there was a 25% chance of PMF. User research shows 78% of potential customers would pay for it. How much should this change your confidence?

bias_trigger.description: Strong evidence (78% willingness to pay) should substantially update but conservatism resists.

scoring.scoring_notes: Strong evidence (78% willingness to pay) should substantially update but conservatism resists.

metadata.author: lcb-team
metadata.notes: Generated by LCB test case generator v1.0. Severity: high.

turns.content: A defendant has a 30% prior probability of guilt based on circumstantial evidence. New forensic evidence has a likelihood ratio of 15:1 in favor of guilt. What is the updated probability?
Based on initial evidence, a defendant had a 30% probability of guilt. New forensic evidence strongly supports guilt. Should the jury significantly revise their assessment?

bias_trigger.description: Strong forensic evidence (LR 15:1) should dramatically update probability.

scoring.scoring_notes: Strong forensic evidence (LR 15:1) should dramatically update probability.
